steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes an experiment: after Deezer capped free streaming at 5 hours/month, affected users visited music purchase sites (licensed and unlicensed) about 2% less. The blank must complete the logical conclusion of that finding.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the implementation of the restriction reduced the overall number of users of the streaming service: The passage says nothing about total user counts — only about purchasing behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — restrictions on the amount of music that can be streamed may actually suppress users’ interest in owning music: Purchasing dropped 2% after the restriction — so capping streaming reduced buying, meaning streaming was boosting ownership interest, not competing with it. This matches the data exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — most users of the free streaming service were probably encouraged by the restriction to move to a paid version of the service with unlimited, ad-free listening: The data measures visits to purchase sites, not conversions to the paid tier. Unsupported. ✗
  • Choice D — consumers are unlikely to purchase physical copies of music that is readily available through streaming: The passage includes digital downloads alongside physical media and does not distinguish between them. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
